title: triticum vulgare convar. turgidum
intro: Triticum vulgare convar. turgidum, commonly known as durum wheat, is a subspecies of wheat that is widely cultivated for its high protein content and is used to produce pasta, bread, and other baked goods. The medicinal properties of durum wheat include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and lower cholesterol levels due to its fiber and antioxidant content. The health benefits of consuming durum wheat include improved digestion, reduced risk of heart disease, and increased satiety due to its high fiber content. Durum wheat can be prepared as a herbal tea or used in various forms of herbal medicine, such as tinctures and infusions, to promote overall health and well-being.
